prompt:
A medium-shot authentic daily life photograph, natural candid moment, of an East Asian young woman standing in a low-ceilinged tatami room near dusk. 85mm lens, waist-up framing. The room behind her: worn tatami, a dark clay vase on the floor with a single dried branch, shoji screens glowing faintly with the last of the day's light.
East Asian young woman in her early 20s. Almond-shaped eyes with natural double eyelids, gaze soft and slightly lowered. Straight refined nose. Skin tone fair to light beige, warm with the last evening light. Skin subsurface scattering visible under directional light, specular micro-highlights on cheekbones and nose ridge, fine foundation powder grain perceptible.
She wears a dusty rose silk qipao with hibiscus embroidery in threads of faded burgundy and pale sage — the pattern worn, the rose pigment deepened slightly with age into something closer to old tea-stain pink. Mandarin collar, short sleeves. She stands facing the glowing shoji screens, arms at her sides, entirely still. The room requires no performance from her. Two or three stray hairs displaced by faint air movement, natural unplanned imperfection, not geometrically symmetrical.
The shoji screens glow amber-rose with the last light outside, backlit and softly diffused, casting the room into warm shadow. Her face receives only the reflected warmth — no direct light source visible. The dusty rose and amber shoji occupy the same temperature of color, the room folding her into itself. Subtle ISO 400 film grain in shadow areas, photographic noise texture not CG render smoothness. Aspect ratio 9:16. No watermark, no text overlay, not cartoon, not digitally painted, not illustration, not anime.
